truth: After confirming challenge.protectionSpace.authenticationMethod == NSURLAuthenticationMethodServerTrust, you must still call SecTrustEvaluateWithError(serverTrust, &error) (or the older SecTrustEvaluate) to actually validate the certificate chain — only create and use a URLCredential(trust: serverTrust) if that evaluation succeeds; otherwise call completionHandler(.cancelAuthenticationChallenge, nil). Checking authenticationMethod alone is a routing precondition, not certificate validation — creating the credential without a prior successful SecTrustEvaluateWithError call blindly accepts any server certificate and is vulnerable to MITM.
quote: creates a credential from serverTrust without calling SecTrustEvaluateWithError
